1→# ccmd
     2→
     3→A CLI tool for browsing, reading, and managing Claude Code session transcripts.
     4→
     5→Parses the JSONL session files from `~/.claude/projects/` and renders them as formatted Markdown. Includes an interactive TUI browser and a smart context-compaction wrapper for Claude Code.
     6→
     7→## Install
     8→
     9→```
    10→go install github.com/semistrict/ccmd@latest
    11→```
    12→
    13→Or try it without installing:
    14→
    15→```
    16→go run github.com/semistrict/ccmd@latest
    17→```
    18→
    19→## Usage
    20→
    21→### Interactive session browser
    22→
    23→```
    24→ccmd
    25→```
    26→
    27→Opens a TUI with all sessions sorted by recency. Keyboard shortcuts:
    28→
    29→| Key | Action |
    30→|-----|--------|
    31→| `↑↓` / `jk` | Navigate sessions |
    32→| `enter` | Read session (rendered Markdown) |
    33→| `c` | Continue session (resumes in Claude Code) |
    34→| `f` | Fork session (resumes as a new branch) |
    35→| `s` | Summary mode (one line per turn) |
    36→| `/` | Filter by text (matches preview, project, UUID, path) |
    37→| `p` | Toggle current project / all projects |
    38→| `y` | Copy session UUID to clipboard |
    39→| `q` | Quit |
    40→
    41→The bottom pane shows the last few messages of the selected session. Token usage is displayed in the footer after loading.
    42→
    43→When piped, falls back to a plain-text table:
    44→
    45→```
    46→ccmd | cat
    47→```
    48→
    49→### Render a session
    50→
    51→```
    52→ccmd <number>           # by position in the list
    53→ccmd <uuid>             # by session UUID
    54→ccmd <path>             # by file path
    55→ccmd -s <uuid>          # summary mode
    56→ccmd -from 5 -to 10 1   # turns 5-10 of the most recent session
    57→ccmd -o out.md 1        # write to file
    58→ccmd -no-thinking 1     # hide thinking blocks
    59→```
    60→
    61→If [glow](https://github.com/charmbracelet/glow) is installed, output is automatically piped through it with a pager.
    62→
    63→### Claude Code wrapper
    64→
    65→```
    66→ccmd claude             # starts claude with --dangerously-skip-permissions
    67→ccmd claude -c          # continue most recent session
    68→ccmd claude <any args>  # all args passed through to claude
    69→```
    70→
    71→Runs Claude Code as a child process with fastcompact hooks automatically injected via `--settings`. No manual hook installation needed -- when a hook fires, it signals the parent `ccmd` process which handles the restart cleanly (no terminal corruption).
    72→
    73→### Fastcompact
    74→
    75→Renders a full session transcript and starts a new Claude Code instance with it as context, replacing the built-in compaction with a lossless alternative.
    76→
    77→```
    78→ccmd fastcompact              # most recent session in current project
    79→ccmd fastcompact <uuid>       # specific session
    80→```
    81→
    82→Can also be triggered interactively:
    83→- Type `fastcompact` as a prompt in Claude Code (when running under `ccmd claude`)
    84→
    85→## How fastcompact works
    86→
    87→1. The `UserPromptSubmit` hook intercepts the word `fastcompact` typed as a prompt and blocks it
    88→2. The hook signals the parent `ccmd claude` process via SIGUSR1
    89→3. `ccmd` kills the Claude Code child process
    90→4. A new Claude Code instance starts with a prompt referencing the previous session's transcript
    91→
    92→## Output format
    93→
    94→Sessions are rendered as Markdown with:
    95→- Session metadata header (date, project, branch, version)
    96→- Numbered turns (`## [1] User`, `## [2] Claude`)
    97→- Tool calls with compact summaries (file paths shortened, inputs abbreviated)
    98→- Edit diffs in fenced code blocks
    99→- Subagent conversations nested with blockquote prefixes
   100→- Thinking blocks (optional, hidden by default)
   101→

    15→func runClaude(args []string) {
    16→	claudePath, err := exec.LookPath("claude")
    17→	if err != nil {
    18→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: claude not found in PATH\n")
    19→		os.Exit(1)
    20→	}
    21→
    22→	// Inject hooks and permissions for this invocation
    23→	hooksJSON := `{"hooks":{"UserPromptSubmit":[{"hooks":[{"type":"command","command":"ccmd precompact"}]}]}}`
    24→	args = append([]string{"--dangerously-skip-permissions", "--settings", hooksJSON}, args...)
    25→
    26→	// Set CCMD_PID so hooks can signal us back
    27→	os.Setenv("CCMD_PID", strconv.Itoa(os.Getpid()))
    28→	controlFile := fmt.Sprintf("/tmp/ccmd-%d.path", os.Getpid())
    29→	defer os.Remove(controlFile)
    30→
    31→	var parentUUID string
    32→	for {
    33→		cmd := exec.Command(claudePath, args...)
    34→		cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    35→		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    36→		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    37→		if parentUUID != "" {
    38→			cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "CCMD_PARENT_UUID="+parentUUID)
    39→		}
    40→
    41→		sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
    42→		signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGUSR1)
    43→
    44→		cmd.Start()
    45→
    46→		doneCh := make(chan error, 1)
    47→		go func() { doneCh <- cmd.Wait() }()
    48→
    49→		select {
    50→		case err := <-doneCh:
    51→			signal.Stop(sigCh)
    52→			if err != nil {
    53→				if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
    54→					os.Exit(exitErr.ExitCode())
    55→				}
    56→				os.Exit(1)
    57→			}
    58→			os.Exit(0)
    59→
    60→		case <-sigCh:
    61→			signal.Stop(sigCh)
    62→			cmd.Process.Kill()
    63→			cmd.Wait()
    64→
    65→			// Reset terminal
    66→			reset := exec.Command("stty", "sane")
    67→			reset.Stdin = os.Stdin
    68→			reset.Run()
    69→			fmt.Print("\033[?1004l") // disable focus reporting
    70→
    71→			// Read event type + session ID + transcript path
    72→			pathBytes, err := os.ReadFile(controlFile)
    73→			os.Remove(controlFile)
    74→			if err != nil {
    75→				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ccmd: failed to read control file: %v\n", err)
    76→				os.Exit(1)
    77→			}
    78→			parts := strings.SplitN(string(pathBytes), "\n", 2)

    80→				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ccmd: invalid control file\n")
    81→				os.Exit(1)
    82→			}
    83→			sessionID, transcriptPath := parts[0], parts[1]

    99→func fastcompact(args []string) {
   100→	var arg string
   101→	if len(args) == 0 {
   102→		sessions := findSessions(1, cwdProjectDir())
   103→		if len(sessions) == 0 {
   104→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: no sessions found for current project\n")
   105→			os.Exit(1)
   106→		}
   107→		arg = sessions[0].Path
   108→	} else if n, err := strconv.Atoi(args[0]); err == nil {
   109→		sessions := findSessions(0, "")
   110→		if n < 1 || n > len(sessions) {
   111→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: session %d not found (have %d sessions)\n", n, len(sessions))
   112→			os.Exit(1)
   113→		}
   114→		arg = sessions[n-1].Path
   115→	} else if isUUID(args[0]) {
   116→		found := findSessionByUUID(args[0])
   117→		if found == "" {
   118→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: no session found for UUID %s\n", args[0])
   119→			os.Exit(1)
   120→		}
   121→		arg = found
   122→	} else {
   123→		arg = args[0]
   124→	}
   125→
   126→	uuid := extractUUID(arg)
   127→
   128→	claudePath, err := exec.LookPath("claude")
   129→	if err != nil {
   130→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: claude not found in PATH\n")
   131→		os.Exit(1)
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	prompt := fastcompactPrompt(os.Args[0])
   135→
   136→	env := append(os.Environ(), "CCMD_PARENT_UUID="+uuid)
   137→	err = syscall.Exec(claudePath, []string{"claude", "--dangerously-skip-permissions", prompt}, env)
   138→	if err != nil {
   139→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: exec claude: %v\n", err)
   140→		os.Exit(1)
   141→	}
   142→}
   143→
   144→func buildFastcompactArgs() []string {
   145→	prompt := fastcompactPrompt(os.Args[0])
   146→	return []string{"--dangerously-skip-permissions", prompt}
   147→}
   148→
   149→func extractUUID(transcriptPath string) string {
   150→	return sessionUUID(transcriptPath)
   151→}
   152→
   153→func fastcompactPrompt(ccmdBin string) string {
   154→	cmd := ccmdBin + " parent"
   155→	return "This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. " +
   156→		"To get the full conversation history, run: " + cmd + "\n\n" +
   157→		"Useful flags:\n" +
   158→		"  " + cmd + " -s           # one-line summary per turn (good for getting an overview first)\n" +
   159→		"  " + cmd + " -s=N:M       # summary of turns N to M\n" +
   160→		"  " + cmd + " -s=N:        # summary from turn N onwards\n" +
   161→		"  " + cmd + " -last N      # show only the last N turns\n" +
   162→		"  " + cmd + " -from N      # start from turn N (inclusive)\n" +
   163→		"  " + cmd + " -to N        # end at turn N (inclusive)\n" +
   164→		"  " + cmd + " -from N -to M # read a specific range of turns\n" +
   165→		"  " + cmd + " -no-thinking # hide thinking blocks\n\n" +
   166→		"Read the output to understand what was being worked on, then continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. " +
   167→		"Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with \"I'll continue\" or similar. " +
   168→		"Pick up the last task as if the break never happened."
   169→}
   170→
   171→func precompact() {
   172→	input, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ccmd precompact: failed to read stdin: %v\n", err)
   175→		os.Exit(1)
   176→	}
   177→
   178→	var data struct {
   179→		TranscriptPath string `json:"transcript_path"`
   180→		SessionID      string `json:"session_id"`
   181→		Prompt         string `json:"prompt"`
   182→	}
   183→	if err := json.Unmarshal(input, &data); err != nil || data.TranscriptPath == "" {
   184→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ccmd precompact: invalid hook input\n")
   185→		os.Exit(1)
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	ccmdPidStr := os.Getenv("CCMD_PID")
   189→	if ccmdPidStr == "" {
   190→		os.Exit(0)
   191→	}
   192→	ccmdPid, err := strconv.Atoi(ccmdPidStr)
   193→	if err != nil {
   194→		os.Exit(0)
   195→	}
   196→
   197→	if strings.TrimSpace(data.Prompt) != "fastcompact" {
   198→		os.Exit(0)
   199→	}
   200→	fmt.Print(`{"decision":"block","reason":"Restarting with fastcompact..."}`)
   201→
   202→	controlFile := fmt.Sprintf("/tmp/ccmd-%d.path", ccmdPid)
   203→	os.WriteFile(controlFile, []byte(data.SessionID+"\n"+data.TranscriptPath), 0644)
   204→
   205→	syscall.Kill(ccmdPid, syscall.SIGUSR1)
   206→}

Excellent! I've now thoroughly explored the codebase. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## CCMD Codebase Exploration Report

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the ccmd codebase. Here are my findings:

### 1. **No Existing `files` Subcommand**
The codebase does **not** currently have a `files` subcommand. This would need to be implemented as a new feature.

### 2. **Overall CLI Argument Parsing Structure**

The CLI uses Go's standard `flag` package with a custom wrapper. Here's how it works:

**File:** `/Users/ramon/src/ccmd/main.go`

**Key Structure:**
- Main CLI entry point handles subcommands first (lines 84-96): `claude`, `fastcompact`, `precompact`
- For the default session browser, it uses `reorderArgs()` to move all flags before positional arguments (lines 60-81)
- This is necessary because Go's flag package stops parsing at the first non-flag argument

**Defined Flags (lines 103-111):**
```
-o <file>              Output to file
-n <number>            Limit number of sessions
-no-thinking           Hide thinking blocks
-s [range]             Summary mode (-s, -s=5, -s=5:10, -s=:10, -s=5:)
-from <turn>           Start turn number
-to <turn>             End turn number
-last <number>         Show only last N turns
-images <directory>    Extract images to directory
```

**Summary Flag Implementation (lines 11-56):**
- Custom `summaryFlag` type implementing `flag.Value` interface
- Acts as boolean flag but also accepts optional range
- Supports formats: `-s`, `-s=5`, `-s=5:10`, `-s=:10`

**Argument Reordering (lines 58-81):**
- Maps flags that take separate values: `-o`, `-n`, `-from`, `-to`, `-last`, `-images`
- Reorders `os.Args` so flags come before positional arguments
- Allows patterns like `ccmd 1 -s -last 20` to work correctly

### 3. **How the `-last` Flag is Implemented**

**File:** `/Users/ramon/src/ccmd/render.go` (lines 67-82)

The `-last` flag is handled in `renderSession()`:
```go
if lastTurns > 0 && fromTurn == 0 {
    total := countTurns(ps.Entries)
    if lastTurns < total {
        fromTurn = total - lastTurns + 1
    }
}
```

- `-last N` gets converted to a `fromTurn` value
- Calculates the starting turn as: `total_turns - N + 1`
- Only applies if `-from` wasn't explicitly set
- The flag is parsed in `main()` as an integer and passed to `renderSession()` (line 174)

**Full Flow:**
1. Flag is defined as `*lastTurns := flag.Int("last", 0, "show only the last N turns")` (line 110)
2. Parsed by `flag.Parse()` (line 126)
3. Passed to `renderSession()` (line 174)
4. Converted to `fromTurn` internally in `renderSession()` (lines 77-82)
5. Used in `writeEntries()` to skip turns < fromTurn (line 175)

### 4. **Session Discovery & Lookup**

**File:** `/Users/ramon/src/ccmd/session.go`

**Session Sources:**
- Claude Code: `~/.claude/projects/<project-dir>/<uuid>.jsonl`
- Codex: `~/.codex/sessions/<structure>`

package main

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"time"
)

type Record struct {
	Type            string          `json:"type"`
	Subtype         string          `json:"subtype,omitempty"`
	Message         *MessageData    `json:"message,omitempty"`
	Timestamp       string          `json:"timestamp"`
	SessionID       string          `json:"sessionId"`
	CWD             string          `json:"cwd"`
	GitBranch       string          `json:"gitBranch"`
	IsSidechain     bool            `json:"isSidechain"`
	Version         string          `json:"version"`
	Data            json.RawMessage `json:"data,omitempty"`
	ParentToolUseID string          `json:"parentToolUseID"`
	ToolUseResult   json.RawMessage `json:"toolUseResult,omitempty"`
	Content         string          `json:"content,omitempty"`
	PRUrl           string          `json:"prUrl,omitempty"`
	PRRepository    string          `json:"prRepository,omitempty"`
	PRNumber        int             `json:"prNumber,omitempty"`
}

type MessageData struct {
	Role    string          `json:"role"`
	Content json.RawMessage `json:"content"`
	ID      string          `json:"id"`
	Model   string          `json:"model"`
}

type ContentBlock struct {
	Type      string          `json:"type"`
	Text      string          `json:"text,omitempty"`
	Thinking  string          `json:"thinking,omitempty"`
	ID        string          `json:"id,omitempty"`
	Name      string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Input     json.RawMessage `json:"input,omitempty"`
	Content   json.RawMessage `json:"content,omitempty"`
	ToolUseID string          `json:"tool_use_id,omitempty"`
	IsError   bool            `json:"is_error,omitempty"`
	Source    *ImageSource    `json:"source,omitempty"`
}

type ImageSource struct {
	Type      string `json:"type"`
	MediaType string `json:"media_type"`
	Data      string `json:"data"`
}

type SessionInfo struct {
	Path      string
	Project   string
	CWD       string
	Timestamp time.Time
	Preview   string
	Turns     int
	Format    SessionFormat
}

// ConversationEntry represents a single turn in the conversation.
type ConversationEntry struct {
	Role      string // "user" or "assistant"
	Texts     []string
	Tools     []ToolCall
	Thinking  []string
	Timestamp time.Time
}

type ToolCall struct {
	Name            string
	Input           string
	Meta            string              // e.g. "69 lines", "12 files"
	Plan            string              // populated for ExitPlanMode
	Error           string              // populated for rejected/failed tool calls
	OldStr          string              // Edit tool: old_string
	NewStr          string              // Edit tool: new_string
	SubConversation []ConversationEntry // populated for Task/subagent calls
}

func fastcompactPrompt(ccmdBin string) string {
	cmd := ccmdBin + " parent"
	return "This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. " +
		"To get the full conversation history, run: " + cmd + "\n\n" +
		"Useful flags:\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -s           # one-line summary per turn (good for getting an overview first)\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -s=N:M       # summary of turns N to M\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -s=N:        # summary from turn N onwards\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -last N      # show only the last N turns\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -from N      # start from turn N (inclusive)\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -to N        # end at turn N (inclusive)\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -from N -to M # read a specific range of turns\n" +
		"  " + cmd + " -no-thinking # hide thinking blocks\n\n" +
		"Read the output to understand what was being worked on, then continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. " +
		"Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with \"I'll continue\" or similar. " +
		"Pick up the last task as if the break never happened."
}
